Learning Outcome: Know that the Incident Response Policy exists and is managed by IT/SIRT; incident handling, evidence preservation, communications, and recovery are coordinated by the Incident Commander and SIRT

If you observe something that may be a security incident, report it immediately — raise a flag in Slack #general, DM an IT team member or your manager, or email security@civicactions.com; good-faith reporting is blame-free and will not result in retaliation; do not attempt to investigate or remediate on your own
Handle incident information on a need-to-know basis; do not disclose details outside designated channels; participate in post-incident retrospectives when asked
Direct all incident communications through the Communications Officer using approved channels and templates; keep updates factual, time-stamped, and audience-appropriate

Audience: Managers; IT / Service Desk; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Escalate supplier incidents that may affect CivicActions or client data; understand that contracts (especially those involving CUI/DoD) may require time-bound external reporting
Preserve logs, images, and artifacts with chain of custody; coordinate with SIRT before acquiring, copying, or analyzing data; understand that incident records are Controlled Records
Explain that recovery targets are set by system criticality and the Contingency Plan; understand that emergency changes to restore service must be documented and reviewed after the fact

List the six IRP phases (Breath, Document, Initiate, Assess, Remediate, Conclude); explain that safety and containment come first using reversible, least-privilege, blast-radius-limiting actions; declare incidents and assign severity
Conduct post-incident retrospectives; update the Risk Register and create POA&Ms from lessons learned; explain that personnel-specific follow-ups are handled by PeopleOps, not the IR process

Audience: IT / Service Desk; SIRT members; Managers
Learning Outcome: Understand that violations can result in access revocation, disciplinary action, or contract remedies
